summary: Monster Eyes opener, soaring Bird Song and tight second set
blurb: The show opens with a punchy Greatest Story Ever Told into Touch of Grey before launching into a monster Eyes Of The World → Samson & Delilah sequence. Highlights include magical Bird Song, soaring Let It Grow, and a crisp Truckin' → Spoonful stretch. Brent’s vocals shine on Never Trust A Woman and I Will Take You Home while Jerry’s guitar blazes throughout.
review: March 25, 1990 at the Knickerbocker Arena captures one of the Dead’s late‑era high points. The first set surprises with a rare Greatest Story Ever Told opener that immediately sets an uplifting tone, then rolls into a powerful Touch of Grey and bluesy Wang Dang Doodle before Brent takes center stage on Never Trust A Woman. The second set is where the night truly shines: a monster Eyes Of The World that segues seamlessly into Samson & Delilah, followed by a soaring Bird Song and a lyrical Let It Grow. Jerry’s guitar is “on fire” during those jams, delivering magical solos that lift the entire band. Truckin' > Spoonful rides a tight rhythm section, with Mickey and Bill laying down spacious drumming that lets the music breathe. Brent Mydland’s keyboard work is both supportive and adventurous, especially on I Will Take You Home where his vocal and organ contributions stand out. The show closes with a warm Black Peter and a classic Mighty Quinn encore, leaving fans with a sense of completeness. Among the many sources, the Gorinsky soundboard (gd90-03-25.sbd.gorinsky.8506.sbeok.shnf) offers the clearest, most balanced capture—crisp enough to hear every nuance while preserving the live energy.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: The majority of recordings (6 out of 10) are marked positive, praising the monster Eyes opener, Bird Song, and overall tight band play. Mixed sentiments focus on recording quirks rather than performance flaws. No negative comments about playing were found, so a solid positive adjustment is applied.

best_recording.reason: Excellent soundboard capture with crisp, balanced mix; highlights the monster Eyes opener and magical Bird Song while preserving full band dynamics.

key_highlights: Monster Eyes Of The World → Samson & Delilah sequence that drives the second set
Magical Bird Song and soaring Let It Grow showcasing Jerry’s fire guitar work
Brent Mydland’s vocal and keyboard peaks on Never Trust A Woman and I Will Take You Home
Tight Truckin' → Spoonful stretch with solid drum fills and space

band_performance.Jerry: Guitar blazes throughout—fire on Eyes, magical solos on Bird Song and Let It Grow; occasional vocal slip‑ups but overall a stellar, adventurous performance including experimental sax on Wang Dang Doodle.
band_performance.Phil: Bass provides a solid foundation; no specific criticisms noted, keeping the groove steady under the band’s improvisations.
band_performance.Bob: Rhythm guitar is tight and confident; reviewers note the boys nailed every note, especially during the Truckin' stretch.
band_performance.Brent: Keyboard work shines—driving force under Samson, peak on Take You Home, and standout vocal moments on Never Trust A Woman and I Will Take You Home. Praised as one of his best late‑era performances.
band_performance.Mickey: Drums receive mixed notes; unspectacular at times but step up after Truckin', providing spacious fills that support the jams.
band_performance.Billy: Solid drumming throughout, with a notable fill on Truckin' and reliable timekeeping in the second set.

must_listen_sequences: Goin' Down The Road Feeling Bad
